feedback.text: As a paying citizen that has been buying games for over 15 years i am more and more worried about the state of games and digital distribution and the possible cancelation and permenent removal of games that were payed for , the higher pricing while only allowing digital distribution and only buying a "license" that can be taken away for almost any reason is quite conserning for the future , games have been getting more expensive , while the tehnology is making it easier and cheaper to produce games , and games have almost exclusively been moved to digital only , even disks that you get are mostly just like an installer with everything requiering you to download more from the creators servers which they can shut down for bad reasons if they mess up, which they can hide from the general public till like 1 month of shutting a game down . I would just like to own the product that i buy or that the product can be extended by other means when the creator decides to abandon it. Restoration and continuation of games should be a right as much as you have right to repair or modify your phone when something happens to it instead of having to destroy it just cus its creator doesnt deem it fit for use after a small accident. I would like to see a further addition to the Digital Fairness Act and that is video game ownership.I understand a company can not keep a games servers up forever , but games that can be played alone/singleplayer , and games that we can make private servers for or the servers can be loaded on to somebody new should be a standard , or if i pay the always higer game prices of 70 and higher , a compensation for them shuting it down that can be cashed in , and not just come gift cupon for others games that they can shut down must be implemented , because without it the developers make games unoptimized and in a lazy way they waste a ton of money with no real downsides to the company cus it always somehow get cut down to us taxpayers. PLEASE stop killing games!
